model_output_raw_text: {"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Increase Adam learning-rate to improve early optimization within the fixed 256-step budget; this is a minimal high-leverage change for the MLP regime.","secondary_modes":[]}

{"declared_mode":"indexing","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"OPTIMIZER = \"adamw\"","old":"OPTIMIZER = \"adam\"","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"indexing","rationale":"Switch to AdamW for decoupled weight decay, which often improves early-step generalization for MLPs under short fixed-step training budgets.","secondary_modes":["caching"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Reduce learning rate to improve stability and generalization for AdamW within the 256-step budget; 1e-3 appears too aggressive in this setup.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Activate the cosine schedule now that it is tied to the fixed training horizon, to improve late-step refinement without adding complexity.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 1e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"Reduce regularization strength to improve fit in the short 256-step regime while keeping AdamW’s decoupled decay.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"summaries","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = False","old":"USE_LR_SCHEDULE = True","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"summaries","rationale":"Disable the short-horizon cosine schedule, which consistently degraded validation loss in this run; reverting to constant LR should restore the stronger regime.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 64","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Increase batch size to reduce gradient noise and expose more unique examples per fixed training step, which can improve validation loss in step-limited training.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 128","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Push batch size higher to further increase data coverage per fixed update and improve gradient estimate quality in the step-limited regime.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 256","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Test a larger batch to further improve signal-to-noise and example coverage per fixed update, which has been strongly beneficial so far in this step-limited setting.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 512","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Continue the successful large-batch trend by increasing batch size again, targeting lower-variance updates and better use of the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 5e-4","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"With very large-batch training now in place, increase learning rate to better match the reduced gradient noise and speed progress within the fixed 256-step budget.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 1024","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Test an even larger batch since larger batches have repeatedly improved this fixed-step workload, and LR is already scaled up to support it.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 2048","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Extend the successful trend by increasing batch size again; with fixed steps, this raises dataset coverage per update and may further reduce validation loss.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 8192","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 4096","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Probe whether moving closer to full-batch behavior further improves optimization under the fixed update budget, building on the consistent gains from larger batches.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"caching","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 0.0","old":"WEIGHT_DECAY = 5e-5","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"caching","rationale":"At very large batch and many effective passes, test removing weight decay to reduce underfitting pressure and improve final validation loss.","secondary_modes":["indexing"]}

{"declared_mode":"micro","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"BATCH_SIZE = 10000","old":"BATCH_SIZE = 8192","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"micro","rationale":"Try a modest additional batch-size increase toward full-batch updates, following the strong monotonic gains from larger batches so far.","secondary_modes":["topk"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Apply a small LR increase to better exploit the very large-batch regime while keeping the change conservative near the current optimum.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.3e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.2e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Take one more small LR step upward in the same successful large-batch regime to test for additional optimization headroom.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}

{"declared_mode":"topk","edit_format":"structured_edits","edits":[{"anchor":"","function":"","new":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.4e-3","old":"LEARNING_RATE = 1.3e-3","op":"replace_exact","source":"","text":""}],"primary_mode":"topk","rationale":"Use the last step to test one final incremental LR increase in the same high-batch regime that has consistently produced improvements.","secondary_modes":["micro"]}
